rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assume/guarantee expressions are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared.
Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annex expressions to avoid type resolution errors.
Align helper declaration types with the types of the expressions they represent to prevent assumed vs actual type mismatches.
Avoid referencing AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as typed features.
Ensure that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are either AADL features or locally declared eq helpers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax requires `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` clauses to be properly typed and scoped within the annex.
Output range guarantees should use boolean helper functions to express value bounds.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL package-qualified identifiers or property names that are not visible in the current scope.
Do not mix AADL timing properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) directly into AGREE expressions without explicit typed exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage
